model.label: Claude Opus 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Hybrid reasoning model that pushes the frontier for coding and AI agents, featuring a 1M context window.

pick.rationale: Gonzaga is a dominant 3-seed ranked 11th nationally in both KenPom and Torvik with a 30-3 record and an elite defense ranked 9th (AdjD 93.9), while Kennesaw State is a 14-seed C-USA Tournament champion with a 21-13 record and an SRS of just 0.13 (151st nationally). Although Gonzaga is missing Braden Huff due to a dislocated kneecap suffered in January, the Bulldogs have thrived without him for months, and their adjusted efficiency margin of +28.16 vastly exceeds Kennesaw State's modest profile built against a weak schedule (SOS 184th). The talent and efficiency gap across every dimension makes this one of the more lopsided 3-14 matchups in the bracket, and Gonzaga should advance comfortably.

reasoningStep.title: Gonzaga vs Kennesaw State – West 3/14 Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Gonzaga's elite defense (9th nationally) and overall efficiency margin of +28.16 create an insurmountable gap over Kennesaw State's 21-13 C-USA profile. Even without injured big man Braden Huff, the Bulldogs have been dominant all season and should handle this matchup with ease.
evidence: Gonzaga: KenPom #11, Torvik #11, 30-3 record, AdjEM +28.16, AdjO 122.1 (30th), AdjD 93.9 (9th)
Kennesaw State: 21-13 record, SRS 0.13 (151st), SOS -2.00 (184th), ORtg 114.2 (84th), DRtg 104.2 (112th), C-USA Tournament champion
Braden Huff (Gonzaga) out with dislocated kneecap since January – team went 30-3 despite his absence
3-seeds historically beat 14-seeds approximately 85%+ of the time, and this matchup features a wider quality gap than typical

Gonzaga’s NCAA Tournament path only grows more treacherous the longer Braden Huff sits out | Dave Boling March 15, 2026 Updated Mon., March 16, 2026 at 3:44 p.m. X Email Reddit SMS By Dave Boling The Spokesman-Review My on-site scouting report on Gonzaga’s Braden Huff last week: Despite a stubborn knee injury suffered in January, Huff was still able to elevate and was dangerous at the rim. Visual evidence. Following the West Coast Conference Tournament title game, Huff was able to climb up a ladder and snip off a portion of the net at the rim with a pair of scissors. Beyond that? He was still unable to suit up and actually play. The GU star forward’s injured leg has already pushed past its estimated range of repair of between four and eight weeks. Entering the ninth week of recovery, Huff has been ruled out for the opening weekend of the tournament, starting Thursday at Portland’s Moda Center against No. 14-seed Kennesaw State . Their No. 3 seed, and opening weekend venue in Portland, is news that couldn’t be better for GU. But Huff’s absence affects the Zags’ future, and his doubt creates a bittersweet aftertaste during this time when celebration is the mode of the moment. Bitter? Obvious. Wondering what t
